key_insight: The paper shows that kernel-based operator learning can be analyzed as a two-stage process, yielding explicit rules for how training pairs, input samples, and output resolution must be balanced to control total error.
engineering_relevance: It provides practical scaling laws and a physics-informed reconstruction method that can improve surrogate models for PDE-driven systems without retraining for each new input.
method: Offline kernel regression learns a discretized operator from input-output training pairs.
Online kernel reconstruction recovers the output function from predicted observations.
A PDE-residual penalty is added at evaluation time to enforce physics-informed consistency.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports numerical experiments that validate the error analysis and show the physics-informed reconstruction strategy is effective.
watch_item: Performance depends on careful coupling of budget parameters and on choosing collocation points/residual penalties well; poor settings may weaken reconstruction accuracy.
paper_title: Kernel-based Operator Learning: Error Analysis, Budget Allocation, and a Physics-Informed Extension
